title: xylosma suaveolens subsp. gracilis
intro: Xylosma suaveolens subsp. gracilis, also known as the sweet-leaved xylosma, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to Australia and New Guinea, valued for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Xylosma suaveolens subsp. gracilis have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, and it is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
